tasks.task: Read the whole crate. Add a small end-to-end settings parser: expose `parse_setting(input: &str) -> Option<Setting>` where `Setting` has `key` and `value` strings. It should parse `name=value`, trim whitespace, reject empty keys, and pass the existing tests. Complete only after cargo test passes.

validators.name: cargo_test
validators.detail:
warning: type `Setting` is more private than the item `parse_setting`
  --> src/lib.rs:11:1
   |
11 | pub fn parse_setting(input: &str) -> Option<Setting> {
   | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ function `parse_setting` is reachable at visibility `pub`
   |
note: but type `Setting` is only usable at visibility `pub(crate)`
  --> src/lib.rs:6:1
   |
 6 | struct Setting {
   | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
   = note: `#[warn(private_interfaces)]` on by default

error[E0616]: field `key` of struct `bench_vertical::Setting` is private
 --> tests/settings_tests.rs:6:24
  |
6 |     assert_eq!(setting.key, "name");
  |                        ^^^ private field

error[E0616]: field `value` of struct `bench_vertical::Setting` is private
 --> tests/settings_tests.rs:7:24
  |
7 |     assert_eq!(setting.value, "Ada");
  |                        ^^^^^ private field

For more information about this error, try `rustc --explain E0616`.
